> **Before you start**: do every migration with the **server stopped**, and fully close the source game/server too. Palworld writes saves continuously while running — a half-copied file is a broken file.

## The most important concept

The server does not automatically load "the one" world folder. It reads `DedicatedServerName=` in `GameUserSettings.ini` and looks for the folder with that GUID. Nine out of ten failed migrations are this line not matching — the server can't find the world and silently starts a brand-new one.

Good news: on the GUI's **Saves & backups** tab, every world's GUID and "active" status is listed clearly. Clicking "Set as active world" fixes that line for you — no manual editing.

## Scenario A: from another dedicated server

The simplest case — the format is identical.

1. On the **source**, stop the old server, go to `Pal/Saved/SaveGames/0/`, find your world folder (an alphanumeric GUID) and zip the whole thing.
2. On the **target**, after creating the instance in the GUI, **start it once and stop it** — Palworld only generates saves and config files on first launch.
3. GUI → **Mods** tab → "Browse all" → navigate to `Pal/Saved/SaveGames/0/` and upload the .sav files inside the GUID folder.
4. GUI → **Saves & backups** tab → find the uploaded world → click "**Set as active world**".
5. Start the server; players join with their original characters.

> **Do not copy the old `GameUserSettings.ini` directly.** It carries the old host's PublicIP and RCON settings, breaking server-list display or remote management. Only `DedicatedServerName` needs changing — and the GUI does that for you.

## Scenario B: from v1 palserver-GUI

The `Pal/Saved/` structure under v1 is exactly the same as scenario A — follow those steps.

If v1 is on the same machine, it's even easier to **adopt in place**: when creating the instance, set "existing server directory" to the v1 server folder (the level containing `PalServer.exe`). World, mods and settings are all taken over in place — no file moving.

## Scenario C: from local co-op (4-player invite code)

The only scenario that hits **character reset**: local saves encode PlayerUid differently from dedicated servers, so moving players over directly forces them to recreate characters (Pals and buildings remain; the character is gone).

1. Find the local save: `%LOCALAPPDATA%\Pal\Saved\SaveGames\<your SteamID>\<world GUID>\`
2. Upload it under the server's `SaveGames/0/` as in scenario A.
3. Use the community tool [palworld-host-save-fix](https://github.com/xNul/palworld-host-save-fix) to convert the host player's .sav to dedicated-server format (you need the old host save's filename, and each player must join the server once to generate their new PlayerUid).
4. Back in the GUI: "Set as active world" → start.

> No tool guarantees a 100% successful host-character transfer. **Click "Back up now" in the GUI before touching anything** — one-click restore if things go wrong.

## Post-migration checklist

- On the **Saves & backups** tab, your world shows "Active"
- World size looks right (an empty world is usually under 1 MB; a played world starts at tens of MB)
- No `Save data is corrupted` in the logs after starting
- In-game, buildings, Pals and levels are all there

## FAQ

**It's a brand-new world; everything is gone** — `DedicatedServerName` doesn't point at your GUID. Go to Saves & backups and click "Set as active world".

**The world is there but I must recreate my character** — scenario C's PlayerUid issue, see step 3 above; if it's server-to-server, check the `Players/` folder wasn't missed.

**`Save data is corrupted` on start** — the source wasn't shut down cleanly during the copy, or the transfer was truncated. Restore from backup and copy again.

**Cross-platform (Windows ↔ Linux)** — works; the `.sav` format is identical. Config files live in `Config/WindowsServer/` vs `Config/LinuxServer/` respectively, and the GUI handles that per platform automatically.

官方註記:會增加磁碟負載。存檔碟是慢速硬碟時建議關閉;官方警告慢速儲存可能損毀存: Official note: increases disk load. Consider disabling on slow HDDs; the devs warn slow storage may corrupt saves — an SSD is recommended.
高負載伺服器社群建議 300–600 秒可減少存檔卡頓;代價是異常關機時回檔的損: For heavily loaded servers the community suggests 300–600 s to reduce autosave stutter; the trade-off is losing more progress on an unexpected shutdown.
每影格處理的公會數:越高公會更新越即時,CPU 成本也越高。: Guilds processed per frame: higher keeps guilds more up to date at a higher CPU cost.
官方註記:調高會提高伺服器處理負載;高負載伺服器社群建議降到 10。: Official note: raising this increases server load; for heavily loaded servers the community suggests lowering it to 10.
掉落物是重載伺服器的主要負擔之一;高負載伺服器社群建議 2000–2500。: Dropped items are one of the main loads on busy servers; the community suggests 2000–2500 for heavily loaded servers.
掉落物存活時數;高負載伺服器社群建議 0.5–1 小時,加快世界清理。: Hours dropped items persist; the community suggests 0.5–1 h on heavily loaded servers to clean up the world faster.
啟用物理計算的掉落物上限(-1 = 無上限);設上限可減少物理運算負擔。: Cap on physics-enabled dropped items (-1 = unlimited); setting a cap reduces physics load.
怕魯/玩家的同步距離(公分):調低可減少同步負擔,但玩家會較晚看到遠處的生物。: Replication distance for pals/players (cm): lower reduces sync load, but players see distant creatures later.
清空指定玩家背包(或指定容器)的物品,可減少世界殘留物件與存檔量。: Clears the items in a player's inventory (or a specific container); reduces leftover world objects and save size.
依條件批次刪除玩家的帕魯(例如清理離線玩家的過量帕魯),減輕伺服器物件負擔。: Batch-deletes a player's pals by filter (e.g. trimming offline players' excess pals) to lighten server object load.
刪除離指定座標最近的據點,可清除無主/廢棄據點;PalDefender 刪除前會: Deletes the base nearest to the given coordinates — useful for clearing abandoned bases; PalDefender writes a JSON backup of the base before deletion.

門檻以帕魯專用伺服器需求為準:記憶體吃最兇(單實例 8-20GB),tick 吃: Thresholds follow real Palworld server needs: RAM is the biggest eater (8-20GB per instance), tick rate is single-core bound, auto-backups need sequential writes. Network is an outbound proxy metric, not actual player latency.

頭目狀態需玩家經過附近才會更新,但看過之後會記住(玩家離開不會變回未知)。野外頭: Boss status only updates when a player passes nearby, but it's remembered afterward (it won't revert to unknown when the player leaves). Overworld bosses respawn on in-game time with no fixed timer, so they always show "respawns around the next in-game day" without a countdown; dungeon boss respawn times are game-native and precise.
頭目回報模組已安裝,伺服器下次啟動後開始回報(每 15 秒更新)。: Boss reporter mod installed. It starts reporting after the next server restart (updates every 15 seconds).

目前沒有重生中的地城頭目。: No dungeon bosses are respawning right now.
